questions.text: An angle which is greater than 180° but less than 360° is called:
questions.explanation: By definition, a reflex angle lies between 180 and 360 degrees.

questions.text: If the sum of two angles is 90°, then they are called:
questions.explanation: Complementary angles add up to a right angle (90°).

questions.text: If a ray stands on a line, then the sum of the two adjacent angles so formed is:
questions.explanation: This is the Linear Pair Axiom; angles on a straight line sum to 180°.

questions.text: Two lines are parallel to the same line. These two lines are ______ to each other.
questions.explanation: Lines parallel to the same line are parallel to each other (Transitive property of parallelism).

questions.text: The sum of all the angles of a triangle is:
questions.explanation: The Angle Sum Property of a triangle states the total internal angles equal 180°.

questions.text: If one angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the other two angles, then the triangle is:
questions.explanation: x + (y + z) = 180. If x = y + z, then 2x = 180, so x = 90°.

questions.text: If two interior angles on the same side of a transversal intersecting two parallel lines are in the ratio 2:3, the greater angle is:
In a triangle, an exterior angle is equal to the sum of its:
questions.explanation: The Exterior Angle Theorem states it equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

questions.text: Vertically opposite angles are always:
questions.explanation: When two lines intersect, the angles opposite the vertex are identical.

questions.text: If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, then the alternate interior angles are:
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of parallel lines cut by a transversal.

questions.text: The complement of 55° is:
The supplement of 105° is:
An angle is equal to its supplement. The angle is:
An angle is 1/4 of its complement. Find the angle:
How many lines can pass through a single point?
questions.explanation: Infinite lines can be drawn originating from or passing through a single point.

questions.text: Two angles form a linear pair. If one is acute, the other must be:
questions.explanation: If x < 90, then 180 - x > 90. Therefore, the other must be obtuse.

questions.text: In a right triangle, the sum of the two acute angles is:
questions.explanation: 180 - 90 (the right angle) = 90 degrees.

questions.text: If three or more points lie on the same line, they are called:
questions.explanation: Collinear means 'on the same line'.

questions.text: The angle between the bisectors of two adjacent supplementary angles is:
If two lines intersect, and one of the vertically opposite angles is 40°, find the other three angles:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite is 40. Linear pair is 180 - 40 = 140. Its opposite is also 140.

questions.text: The sum of angles at a point is:
questions.explanation: Angles around a complete point rotation sum to 360 degrees.

questions.text: If two lines are intersected by a transversal, and the corresponding angles are equal, the lines are:
questions.explanation: This is the converse of the corresponding angles axiom.

questions.text: An exterior angle of a triangle is 110° and its two interior opposite angles are equal. Each of these angles is:
The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 2:4:3. The smallest angle of the triangle is:
If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, then the sum of consecutive interior angles is:
questions.explanation: Consecutive interior angles (co-interior) are supplementary.

questions.text: A line joining two points is called a:
questions.explanation: A line segment has two fixed endpoints.

questions.text: A ray has ______ end point(s).
questions.explanation: A ray starts at one point and extends indefinitely in one direction.

questions.text: Angles which have a common vertex and a common arm but no common interior points are:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of adjacent angles.

questions.text: If two lines are perpendicular to the same line, they are:
questions.explanation: In a plane, two lines perpendicular to a third line must be parallel.

questions.text: The measure of a straight angle is:
questions.explanation: A straight angle forms a straight line.

questions.text: The measure of a complete angle is:
questions.explanation: A full rotation is 360 degrees.

questions.text: Two adjacent angles sum to 180°. They form a:
questions.explanation: If adjacent angles are supplementary, their non-common arms form a line (linear pair).

questions.text: If one angle of a triangle is 130°, then the angle between the bisectors of the other two angles is:
A triangle can have ______ obtuse angle(s).
questions.explanation: If it had two, the sum would exceed 180°.

questions.text: The internal bisectors of the angles of a triangle are:
questions.explanation: They meet at a single point called the incenter.

questions.text: Find the angle which is four times its complement:
An angle whose measure is exactly 90° is:
questions.explanation: Standard definition of a right angle.

questions.text: A line segment has ______ length.
questions.explanation: A line segment is measurable between its two endpoints.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel, they ______ intersect.
questions.explanation: Parallel lines maintain a constant distance and never cross.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, if AB = AC and angle B = 50°, then angle C is:
questions.explanation: Angles opposite to equal sides of a triangle are equal.
